_comment: Worked example of the 'base + incremental-with-cap' shape (radiotherapy): a base course at a fixed rate, then each additional fraction priced separately up to a hard cap. This is what a flat per_X rate cannot express.

additional_information.note: Billed as one full radical course package, not per fraction. Additional fractions beyond the course use MR001D.
additional_information.rate_caveat: Exact tier mapping is ambiguous from the PDF extraction (figures 12100 / 23100 / 27300 appear in the row); confirm against the clean HBP 2022 master Excel.

source_refs.rates_inr / base_rate_inr: HBP-2022.pdf master rows MR001A (Radical 23100 ... 27300; 12100 also printed in row)
source_refs.additional_information.rate_caveat: PDF extraction of HBP-2022.pdf — tier columns not cleanly separable for this row

additional_information.note: Each additional fraction beyond the base course is paid at Rs.500/fraction, up to 18 additional fractions (cap Rs.9000). Booked on top of MR001A.

source_refs.pricing.increment: HBP-2022.pdf master row MR001D — 'Additional fractions - 18, Max upto (Every additional fraction at Rs.500 per fraction)'; row shows 500 (18) and 9000
